title: Container restart fails with "address already in use"
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Restarting a podman rootless container results in `address already in use` errors for me. It does not happen always (and maybe the way the image works might somehow cause that, but imo podman should handle that)

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
➜  ~ podman run -d -p 8000:8000 --name wiki crazymax/dokuwiki:edge
ba5b7ab5b59d3c84c05dcc795a3cb81247956aa7d31067538501daa5339d0cd9
➜  ~ podman restart wiki
Error: rootlessport listen tcp 0.0.0.0:8000: bind: address already in use
➜  ~ podman restart wiki
ba5b7ab5b59d3c84c05dcc795a3cb81247956aa7d31067538501daa5339d0cd9
➜  ~ podman restart wiki
ba5b7ab5b59d3c84c05dcc795a3cb81247956aa7d31067538501daa5339d0cd9
➜  ~ podman restart wiki
Error: rootlessport listen tcp 0.0.0.0:8000: bind: address already in use
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

Proper restarts all the time.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.15.5
Built:        Tue Dec  8 15:37:50 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  buildahVersion: 1.18.0

  cpus: 8

    version: "33"
  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: apollo13
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.10.15-200.fc33.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 8343269376
  memTotal: 16419074048
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-0.17-1.fc33.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.17
      commit: 0e9229ae34caaebcb86f1fde18de3acaf18c6d9a
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL
  os: linux

    exists: true

  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns

      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3
      libseccomp: 2.5.0
  swapFree: 38654697472

        FUSE library version 3.9.3
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.31
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-2.2.1-1.fc33.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

No, can retest as soon as podman 3 hits fedora 33

comments.body: Stringly suspect this is the same issue as https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/9083

same here with containers running in a pod and podman version: 
```
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.14
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```
Any chance you can retest with Conmon 2.0.27 (just released)? It fixed a similar issue for root Podman, so I think it might also fix this
Will fedora 33 get an update to conmon .27? Can you link me to the relevant conmon commit that should fix this issue?

I suspect that https://github.com/containers/conmon/pull/241 may have fixed it. 2.0.27 will definitely come to F33, but I'm not sure of timeframe - we're doing a Podman bump to v3.1.0 later this week and it may get packaged up at part of that.
Ok, if that is early enough as a test for you, I'll happily report back then.
it is going through the pipeline now https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2021-a5f4e3fb1a , probably a couple of days to get to stable
Is there a workaround for this at all?

workarounds that kind of work but I'm sure have significant drawbacks:

* rolling back to conmon 2.0.22, or
* running `pkill containers-rootlessport`

Had anyone confirmed the new Conmon does resolve the issue? It should be
ready for testing now.

On Tue, Mar 16, 2021 at 15:30 sdhoward ***@***.***> wrote:

> workarounds that kind of work but I'm sure have significant drawbacks:
>
>    - rolling back to conmon 2.0.22, or
>    - running pkill containers-rootlessport
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you commented.
>
>
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/9447#issuecomment-800544727>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCFWOKDAQ4NZEVIFFVTTD6WTRANCNFSM4X5YANSQ>
> .
>

@mheon Maybe not related but even after patching to the latest podman and conmon on CentOS7 I keep hitting:

`Failed to set up listener: SocketException: Address already in use`

https://github.com/minhhungit/mongodb-cluster-docker-compose for example doesn't seem to run on Podman.

Packages installed:

podman-3.0.1-2.el7.3.1.x86_64
podman-docker-3.0.1-2.el7.3.1.noarch
podman-plugins-3.0.1-2.el7.3.1.x86_64
conmon-2.0.27-1.el7.1.1.x86_64
buildah-1.19.6-3.el7.4.1.x86_64

And I'm running everything rootless.

> Had anyone confirmed the new Conmon does resolve the issue? It should be ready for testing now.

Thanks for the heads up, I pulled in `conmon-2:2.0.27-1.fc34.x86_64` and it seems fine for now.
I'm not on red hat but I upgraded from conmon 2.0.26 to 2.0.27 and it fixed the issue for me as well -- I no longer have the `bind: address already in use` error.
Closing since this seems to be fixed in the main branches.

Hi there,
Is this expected to be fixed in Podman 3.4.4 (which is the version from the Ubuntu 22.04 repo)?
This issue was closed on Mar 18 2021, and v3.4.4 was released on Dec 8 2021.

It doesn't work for me (but works when using `sudo`).
I'm using WSL2 with Systemd.

Thanks for looking into it :-) 

```
$ sudo apt  install podman-docker
...

$ podman run --rm -p 80:80 httpd:alpine
AH00558: httpd: Could not reliably determine the server's fully qualified domain name, using 10.0.2.100. Set the 'ServerName' directive globally to suppress this message
AH00558: httpd: Could not reliably determine the server's fully qualified domain name, using 10.0.2.100. Set the 'ServerName' directive globally to suppress this message
[Sat Feb 25 15:03:07.649551 2023] [mpm_event:notice] [pid 1:tid 140082517875528] AH00489: Apache/2.4.55 (Unix) configured -- resuming normal operations
[Sat Feb 25 15:03:07.649936 2023] [core:notice] [pid 1:tid 140082517875528] AH00094: Command line: 'httpd -D FOREGROUND'
^C[Sat Feb 25 15:03:09.907730 2023] [mpm_event:notice] [pid 1:tid 140082517875528] AH00491: caught SIGTERM, shutting down

$ podman run --rm -p 80:80 httpd:alpine
Error: rootlessport listen tcp 0.0.0.0:80: bind: address already in use

$ podman --version
podman version 3.4.4

$ cat /etc/os-release
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 22.04.1 LTS"
...

$ uname -a
Linux HOSTNAME 5.15.79.1-microsoft-standard-WSL2 #1 SMP Wed Nov 23 01:01:46 UTC 2022 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

Same issue as @heidemn. Using latest Podman (4.2.2) on Ubuntu 22.04. After restarting a container encountering the following error: `rootlessport listen tcp 0.0.0.0:42193: bind: address already in use`
Please open a new issue, this one is two years old

Apologies @mheon. FYI I've just checked and I wasn't using the latest podman, it was using 3.4.x. I've just tested the latest ("Debian Testing/Bookworm") version and these issues are resolved. How are we meant to get these fixes in production without using such unstable channels? This issue that was resolved 2 years isn't widely available

For others stumbling into this - after I followed these instructions: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/12983
* `$ sudo apt install dbus-user-session`
* `> wsl --shutdown`

...I could no longer reproduce the issue `bind: address already in use`.

> For others stumbling into this - after I followed these instructions: #12983
> 
>     * `$ sudo apt install dbus-user-session`
> 
>     * `> wsl --shutdown`
> 
> 
> ...I could no longer reproduce the issue `bind: address already in use`.

Thanks for the tip. This worked for me on WSL2.
